title: roasted, pillowy Moroccan beef Harira
description: A rich and savory Moroccan stew featuring thinly sliced beef flank steak, perfect for a hearty dinner or special occasion.
instructions: Season the beef flank steak with salt, black pepper, and cumin. Roast the beef in the oven for about 20 minutes, or until browned and cooked to medium-rare.
Thinly slice the roasted beef against the grain.
In a large Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the sliced onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the pot and cook for an additional minute.
Add the chicken broth, cumin, coriander, paprika, and cayenne pepper to the pot. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 10 minutes, or until the flavors have melded together.
Stir in the honey, lemon juice, parsley, and cilantro. Cook for an additional 2 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened slightly.
Serve the Harira hot, garnished with additional parsley and cilantro if desired.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve hot, garnished with additional parsley and cilantro if desired.
Refrigerate or freeze leftovers for later use.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
